pass_summary: All 13 tasks are implemented with high compliance — every planned artifact is present and non-stub. Two issues worth addressing: a silent-drop bug in prompt-context.ts (sessionTitle without context is swallowed), and an indentation defect introduced into file-length.test.ts.

issues.title: sessionTitle silently dropped when context is null but spec is active
issues.description: In src/hooks/prompt-context.ts main(), the output dispatch is: `if (context && sessionTitle)` → emit both, `else if (context)` → emit context only. There is no branch for `sessionTitle && !context`, which can happen when a PENDING/IN_PROGRESS spec exists but `buildSpecContext()` returns null (e.g., findActivePlan diverges in edge cases, or the plan has no tasks). The sessionTitle is silently dropped in that case. The plan spec (Task 3 DoD) states 'Hook output contains hookSpecificOutput when active spec exists' — this case violates that contract.
issues.suggested_fix: Add an `else if (sessionTitle)` branch in src/hooks/prompt-context.ts main(): `else if (sessionTitle) { output({ hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: 'UserPromptSubmit', additionalContext: '', sessionTitle } }); }`. Also add a test case: ACTIVE_PLAN with no tasks → buildSpecContext returns null → buildSessionTitle returns 'spec:...' → output still includes sessionTitle.

issues.title: Indentation defect in file-length.test.ts closes outer describe block prematurely
issues.description: The diff for src/utils/file-length.test.ts shows the existing 'should work without content arg' test was dedented out of its parent describe block (closing brace moved), and the PATH_EXEMPTIONS describe() is appended followed by a bare closing `});` — meaning there is an extra dangling closing brace. This is a structural parse error that may cause the test suite to fail or produce misleading output. The original indentation used 4 spaces inside the outer describe; the replacement uses 2 spaces and closes the outer `describe` prematurely.
issues.suggested_fix: Re-examine src/utils/file-length.test.ts around the 'should work without content arg' test and the PATH_EXEMPTIONS describe block. Restore correct nesting: the 'should work without content arg' test should close its parent describe with a matching `});`, then PATH_EXEMPTIONS is a sibling describe at the same indentation level, with the outer describe file closed only once at the very end.

issues.title: buildSessionTitle test missing IN_PROGRESS case
issues.description: The plan's Task 3 DoD requires 'Unit test covers all 3 states: active spec, no spec, verified spec' — the tests cover PENDING (active), empty dir (no spec), and VERIFIED. However, the SESSION_TITLE_STATUSES set includes both PENDING and IN_PROGRESS. The IN_PROGRESS case is defined as valid but untested in buildSessionTitle tests. An IN_PROGRESS spec with the correct status should return a sessionTitle; this path goes untested.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a fourth test to the buildSessionTitle describe block: write IN_PROGRESS_PLAN (already defined as a fixture at line 60 of prompt-context.test.ts) and assert buildSessionTitle returns 'spec:2026-03-17-in-progress'.

issues.title: getDbPath() calls mkdirSync before accessSync — side effect on failure path
issues.description: In src/memory/config.ts getDbPath(), the code calls mkdirSync(pluginData, { recursive: true }) THEN accessSync(pluginData, W_OK). If mkdirSync succeeds but the directory ends up not writable (e.g., created by root, or a race condition), the directory is created on disk even though the function will fall back to the default path. This is a minor side effect: a partially-created plugin data dir left behind when the env var points to a non-writable location.
issues.suggested_fix: Check writability before creating: first try accessSync on pluginData. If it throws (doesn't exist), try mkdirSync, then re-check with accessSync. This avoids creating dirs that will never be used. Alternatively, document the current behavior as intentional (pre-create the dir and rely on the accessSync to catch truly unwritable cases).

issues.title: Task 7 divergence doc: verify claim references no version number
issues.description: The sentinal-opencode-rules.md file states 'OpenCode does NOT support paths: frontmatter' and references '2026-04-21 (OpenCode docs review)' as the verification date, but does not cite a specific OpenCode version number. The plan's Task 7 pre-check description mentions 'as of v1.3.x' should appear in the docs. Without a version anchor, it's harder to know when to revisit this determination if OpenCode adds paths: support in the future.
issues.suggested_fix: Add the specific OpenCode version that was checked (e.g., 'as of OpenCode v1.3.x') to the 'Last verified' line or the first paragraph of sentinal-opencode-rules.md, so future reviewers know when to re-evaluate.

truths.truth: src/cli/commands/statusline.ts contains git_worktree reference in an extract* function
truths.evidence: extractWorktree() function added at line 122 referencing sessionJson.workspace.git_worktree; workspaceBranch field added to StatuslineInput interface and consumed in formatStatusline()
Diff shows statusLine: { refreshInterval: 30 } added at top level of settings.json; target-assets.test.ts asserts refreshInterval === 30
Diff shows env.CLAUDE_CODE_SESSIONEND_HOOKS_TIMEOUT_MS: '10000' added; test asserts the value
Diff shows plansDirectory: 'docs/plans' added at top level; test asserts value === 'docs/plans'

truths.truth: targets/claude-code/hooks/hooks.json contains 'once': true on memory-restore AND session-start entries (two matches)
truths.evidence: hooks.json shows once: true on the outer hook group objects for both memory-restore (lines 16-24) and session-start (lines 25-34). Placement is on the outer object (not inside the hooks array), which is correct per Claude Code hook schema. Tests assert both entries have once: true and that post-compact-restore does NOT.

truths.truth: src/hooks/prompt-context.ts outputs hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle when active spec exists
truths.evidence: buildSessionTitle() added; main() emits hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle when context && sessionTitle. NOTE: the case where sessionTitle is set but context is null silently drops the sessionTitle — see issue #1.

truths.truth: targets/claude-code/commands/spec-plan.md + spec-master-plan.md frontmatter contains effort: xhigh
truths.evidence: Diff shows effort: high → effort: xhigh in both files; no other frontmatter or body changes
getDbPath() function added in src/memory/config.ts at line 31, reading process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA with writable fallback; 3 tests cover env var unset, set+writable, set+unwritable

truths.truth: All 5 OpenCode standards-*.md files contain paths: frontmatter
truths.evidence: Task 7 took the 7c path: OpenCode does not support paths: frontmatter (verified from docs). The 5 OpenCode standards files were NOT modified. Instead, .sentinal/rules/sentinal-opencode-rules.md was created documenting the divergence. The plan explicitly marks this as the correct outcome for the 7c path — Truth #9 is superseded by the 7c divergence note.
Diff shows validate:plugin script added: 'claude plugin validate targets/claude-code 2>&1 | tee /tmp/sentinal-plugin-validate.log || true'

truths.truth: targets/claude-code/agents/research.md frontmatter contains isolation: worktree AND memory: project
truths.evidence: Diff shows both isolation: worktree and memory: project added to agents/research.md frontmatter; no other changes

truths.truth: src/utils/file-length.ts contains PATH_EXEMPTIONS array with sentinal.ts entry
truths.evidence: PATH_EXEMPTIONS constant added with 'targets/opencode/plugins/sentinal.ts'; checkFileLength() early-returns null for matching paths using endsWith(); unit tests verify both relative and absolute path forms

truths.truth: .sentinal/rules/sentinal-testing.md contains explicit exemption paragraph for sentinal.ts
truths.evidence: Lines 83-92 of sentinal-testing.md contain the exemption section referencing PATH_EXEMPTIONS constant and explaining the OpenCode platform constraint rationale
docs/changelog-adoptions-phase1.md contains all 5 topics as distinct H2 sections with substantive prose (no stubs/TODOs); grep confirms all 5 terms present
